name: Susarón Loop Trail
name_original: Vuelta al Susarón – circular desde Puebla de Lillo por el Alto Porma
description: This moderate 15.4-mile mountain biking loop from Puebla de Lillo offers rewarding views of the Porma reservoir and Cantabrian Mountains.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Puebla De Lillo offers diverse landscapes within the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park. The terrain features majestic mountains, verdant valleys, and significant elevation changes, with peaks like Peña Susarón and Peña Requejines. The area is characterized by native Scots pine forests, ancient beech and birch woods, and the Porma River, which feeds a large reservoir. Two natural glacial lakes, Lago de Isoba and Lago Ausente, also contribute to the varied scenery. Best mountain bike trails around Puebla De Lillo The most popular mountain bike trail is Susarón Loop Trail, a 15.4 miles (24.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of Peña Susarón. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vuelta al Macizo de los Mampodres – Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre desde Puebla de Lillo, a difficult 31.5 miles (50.8 km) path. This route features substantial elevation gain through the regional park. Local mountain bikers also love the Cofiñal Rest Area – View of Puebla de Lillo loop from Puebla de Lillo, a 15.1 miles (24.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Mountain biking around Puebla De Lillo is defined by mountain passes, native pine forests, and glacial lakes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Puebla De Lillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Puebla De Lillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Puebla de Lillo?
A: Puebla de Lillo offers a selection of 11 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. The region is known for its diverse landscapes within the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Puebla de Lillo?
A: The trails around Puebla de Lillo range from moderate to difficult. There are 3 moderate routes and 8 difficult routes, ensuring options for different experience levels. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037751012/susaron-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Susarón Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040463767/vuelta-al-macizo-de-los-mampodres-parque-regional-montana-de-riano-y-mampodre-desde-puebla-de-lillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Macizo de los Mampodres&lt;/a&gt; is considered difficult.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Puebla de Lillo?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Puebla de Lillo are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037751012/susaron-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Susarón Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofiñal Rest Area – View of Puebla de Lillo loop&lt;/a&gt; are both loop trails, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails in Puebla de Lillo traverse a rich natural landscape. You'll encounter majestic mountains like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yordas (1,967 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5543307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Susarón Peak (1,878 m)&lt;/a&gt;, native Scots pine forests, ancient beech and birch woods, and the Porma River. The region also features beautiful glacial lakes such as Lago de Isoba and Lago Ausente.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or viewpoints along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the routes offer various points of interest. You can enjoy panoramic vistas from peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6335140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Pintas 1983 m.&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Pintas&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also pass by traditional structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Traditional Shepherd Hut (Chozo)&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the region's heritage.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Puebla de Lillo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Puebla de Lillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the challenging elevation changes, and the opportunity to immerse themselves in the natural environment of the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Puebla de Lillo?
A: Puebla de Lillo offers stunning views and fresh air throughout the year. While autumn is particularly picturesque with the changing colors of beech and birch forests, the warmer months typically provide the most favorable conditions for mountain biking. During winter, the area transforms into a winter sports hub, so trail conditions may vary significantly.

Q: Are there any facilities for cyclists in Puebla de Lillo?
A: Yes, the municipal sports center in Puebla de Lillo provides a bike repair stand, which can be very useful for riders. They also offer up-to-date information on trail conditions, helping you plan your ride effectively.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The diverse ecosystems of the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park support a rich array of wildlife. You might spot deer, wild boars, foxes, and various rare birds. The region is also home to larger mammals like brown bears, wolves, badgers, roe deer, and capercaillie, though sightings of these are less common.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are several difficult routes with significant distances and elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crestón Tunnel – Texu la Oración loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 61.8 km route with nearly 2,000 meters of elevation gain, offering a substantial adventure.

Q: Can I find accommodation or places to eat in Puebla de Lillo?
A: Puebla de Lillo is a destination for outdoor enthusiasts, and as such, you can find various accommodation options, including rural accommodations. There are also cafes and restaurants in the area where you can refuel after your rides.

Q: Are there any mountain passes accessible by bike in the region?
A: Yes, accessible mountain passes like Puerto de La Tarna and Puerto de las Señales are part of the region's challenging yet rewarding routes. These passes offer spectacular scenery and are often incorporated into longer mountain biking tours.
tips: Beautiful views of the town of Puebla de Lillo when we walk along the picturesque path PR LE-28 of the Ruta de la Cervatina next to the Silvan river.
Nice green area where we will find picnic tables. An ideal place to take a break in a fantastic setting.
Wide and beautiful path between trees located on Monte la Cervatina. Without technical difficulty, it is ideal for enjoying the nature that surrounds us.
From Collado Ferreras we have a beautiful panoramic view of the Porma reservoir or Juan Benet reservoir. This pass gives us access, through a beautiful valley, to the town of Rucayo.
Rolling along the path known as Camposolillo we have magnificent views of the Porma reservoir and behind us (if we roll towards Camposolillo) some beautiful views of Collada de Barbadillo.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The 'Vuelta al Susarón – circular desde Puebla de Lillo por el Alto Porma' mountain biking route typically takes around 2 hours and 23 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace, skill level, and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Expect varied terrain, primarily consisting of 'veredas montañeras' (mountain paths). These can be rocky, with sections of loose stones, sand, and gravel, making them potentially slippery. You'll encounter steep inclines and declines, requiring good technical skills and physical conditioning, especially during the initial ascent.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this mountain biking route is generally considered difficult and not suitable for beginners. The trail involves significant elevation changes, steep sections, and challenging terrain with loose stones. Riders should have good physical conditioning and technical mountain biking skills.

faqs.question: Where does the mountain biking route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one, starting and ending in Puebla de Lillo. You can typically find parking within the village, which serves as a convenient base for accessing the trailhead.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints and natural features along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular panoramic views. Key highlights include a View of Puebla de Lillo, and a stunning View of Embalse del Porma from Collado Ferreras. From higher points, especially around Pico Susarón, you'll enjoy fabulous 360-degree views of the Porma reservoir and the majestic Cantabrian Mountains, passing through forests and valleys within the Regional Park of the Mountains of Riaño and Mampodre.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll ride along parts of the La Cervatina Trail and the Camino de Camposolillo. It also briefly touches upon sections of the Embalse del Porma and Transcantábrica trails.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route is typically during late spring, summer, and early autumn. During these seasons, the weather is generally more stable, and the trails are less likely to be affected by snow or heavy rain, which can make the already challenging terrain even more difficult.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to access this mountain biking route. It is located within the Montañas de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park, which is generally open for public access.

faqs.question: What are the regulations for mountain biking within the Montañas de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park?
faqs.answer: When mountain biking in the Montañas de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park, it's important to stay on marked trails to protect the natural environment. Respect wildlife, carry out all your trash, and be mindful of other trail users. While no specific permits are needed for this route, always check for any temporary restrictions or local regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally dog-friendly. However, given the challenging terrain and potential for encountering wildlife, it's highly recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or steep sections. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a demanding mountain bike ride.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trailhead in Puebla de Lillo?
faqs.answer: Puebla de Lillo offers various amenities for visitors. You can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options within the village, making it a convenient place to refuel or stay overnight before or after your mountain biking adventure.

waypoints.name: View of Embalse del Porma from Collado Ferreras
